title: Oftia africana
intro:
There is no information available on "Oftia africana." However, I can provide information on "Olea africana" which is also known as African olive. 

Olea africana, also known as African olive, is a plant species that belongs to the family Oleaceae. The leaves and fruits of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and it is valued for its numerous health benefits, including the ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Olea africana include oleuropein, hydroxytyrosol, and 3,4-dihydroxyphenylethanol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Olea africana include tea, capsules, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Oftia africana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is native to Africa, where it is found in a variety of habitats including savannas and rocky outcrops. The classification of Oftia africana has undergone changes over the years, reflecting our evolving understanding of plant relationships. Its classification is currently accepted by The Plant List and other botanical authorities. As such, Oftia africana is recognized as a distinct and separate species within the Scrophulariaceae family.
